title: Amid Delivery Pressure, Domino's Drives 20% Increase in Pickup
text: Domino's Pizza's efforts to drive pickup sales are proving successful. The quick-service restaurant (QSR) giant, which has more than 19,500 stores around the world, shared Thursday (Oct. 13) on a call with analysts regarding its third-quarter 2022 financial results that carryout same-store sales grew 19.6% year over year in the quarter.

title: Domino's (DPZ) Stock Up Despite Q3 Earnings & Revenues Miss

title: Domino's Pizza Q3 Results Point To A Positive Turn
text: Domino's shares are reacting favorably to Q3 results even as EPS declined 13% from last year while revenue rose 7%. U.S. same-store sales growth was a bright spot, and management has reduced operating costs to reduce inflation.

title: Domino's Pizza: Down 45% From Highs, Pain Is Baked In
text: The market stinks right now, taking all stocks lower, including Domino's Pizza, Inc. Domino's Pizza has faced rising food input costs and wage/labor inflation, and like many others in service has faced a worker shortage.

title: Domino's Dishes Out Revenue Beat Amid Strong Demand

title: Domino's Pizza (DPZ) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Miss Estimates
text: Domino's Pizza (DPZ) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -5.42% and 0.02%, respectively, for the quarter ended September 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Domino's Pizza shares up 6% premarket after revenue tops estimates in Q3
text: Shares of Domino's Pizza Inc. DPZ, -0.57% soared 6% in premarket trade Thursday, after a third-quarter revenue beat offset weaker-than-expected profit . The company posted net income of $100.5 million, or $2.79 a share, for the quarter, down from $120.4 million, or $3.24 a share, in the year-earlier period.
